name: ExtensionAdverseEvent_Participant
title: R5: Who was involved in the adverse event or the potential adverse event and what they did (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `AdverseEvent.participant` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`AdverseEvent.participant` 0..* `BackboneElement`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `AdverseEvent.participant` 0..* `BackboneElement`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `AdverseEvent.participant` has a context of AdverseEvent based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `AdverseEvent`.
Element `AdverseEvent.participant` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.definition: Distinguishes the type of involvement of the actor in the adverse event, such as contributor or informant.

binding.description: Codes describing the type of involvement of the actor in the adverse event.

element.short: R5: Who was involved in the adverse event or the potential adverse event (new)
element.comment:
Element `AdverseEvent.participant.actor` is part of an existing definition because parent element `AdverseEvent.participant` requires a cross-version extension.
Element `AdverseEvent.participant.actor` has a context of AdverseEvent based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `AdverseEvent`.
Element `AdverseEvent.participant.actor` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
For example, the physician prescribing a drug, a nurse administering the drug, a device that administered the drug, a witness to the event, or an informant of clinical history.

element.short: Who was involved in the adverse event or the potential adverse event
element.definition: Indicates who or what participated in the event.
element.comment: For example, the physician prescribing a drug, a nurse administering the drug, a device that administered the drug, a witness to the event, or an informant of clinical history.
